payload.text: Coral reefs are home to a quarter of all marine species, yet they cover only a tiny part of the ocean floor. When the water grows too warm, the coral loses the tiny algae that feed it and slowly turns white. Scientists are racing to {{b1}} which reefs are most at risk so that they can {{b2}} the healthiest areas before the damage spreads.

payload.text: Sleep is far more active than it looks. While we rest, the brain sorts through the day's events and decides which memories to {{b1}} and which to let fade. Researchers now believe that a poor night's sleep can seriously {{b2}} our ability to concentrate and to make sound decisions the following day.

payload.text: Working from home has changed the daily routine of millions of people. Without a long journey to the office, many workers feel they can {{b1}} their time more freely and spend more of it with their families. Yet managers worry that teams who rarely meet in person may slowly lose the sense of trust that helps a company {{b2}}.

gaps.after: , it proved almost impossible to run in practice.

gaps.explanation: raise doubt で「疑い・不安を生じさせる」。doubt と自然に結びつく動詞は raise。

gaps.before: She spoke to the audience as if she
gaps.explanation: as if + 過去完了で、過去の事実に反する仮定を表す。「長年知っていたかのように」なので had known。

gaps.explanation: raise money で「お金を集める・募る」。fund-raising の定番コロケーション。

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Surprising Mind of the Octopus
paragraphs: For a long time, intelligence was thought to belong mainly to animals with large brains and long lives, such as apes, dolphins, and crows. The octopus, a soft creature that usually lives only a year or two, seemed an unlikely candidate. Yet in aquariums around the world, keepers began to notice that these animals were doing things no simple instinct could explain.

Octopuses have been seen opening jars to reach the food inside, escaping through the smallest gaps in their tanks, and even recognising individual people who visited them each day. What makes their intelligence so strange is that much of it does not sit in a central brain at all. Two-thirds of their nerve cells are spread through their eight arms, so that each arm can taste, touch, and partly decide what to do on its own.

Studying such a different kind of mind forces scientists to rethink what intelligence really is. The octopus reminds us that clever behaviour did not evolve just once, along a single path leading to human beings. Instead it has appeared more than once, in bodies utterly unlike our own, shaped by lives we can barely imagine.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: The octopus shows that intelligence can evolve in very different forms, not only in animals like us.
Octopuses are the most intelligent animals on Earth.
Only animals with large brains and long lives can be intelligent.
Aquarium keepers are wrong to think octopuses can solve problems.

questions.before: Because two-thirds of their nerve cells lie in their arms, each arm can
questions.after: and partly decide what to do on its own.

questions.prompt: オクトパスの知能が「一度きりではなく」進化したことを述べている語句はどれですか。

spans.text: a soft creature that usually lives only a year or two
opening jars to reach the food inside
clever behaviour did not evolve just once, along a single path
keepers began to notice that these animals were doing things

questions.sentence: These abilities are impressive enough on their own.

questions.prompt: 筆者が最後の段落で最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Intelligence can arise more than once and in bodies very different from ours.
Human beings are the only truly intelligent creatures.
Octopuses should be kept in larger tanks in every aquarium.
Scientists have already fully explained how the octopus thinks.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Quiet Power of Walking
paragraphs: Many great thinkers have said that their best ideas came to them not at a desk but on a walk. Writers, scientists, and inventors have all described how a slow stroll seemed to loosen their thoughts and let new connections form. For a long time this sounded like a charming habit rather than a serious claim, but recent studies suggest there may be real truth in it.

When we walk, more blood and oxygen reach the brain, and the gentle, steady movement seems to put the mind into a relaxed but alert state. In one experiment, people were asked to think of creative uses for everyday objects. Those who walked, whether outdoors or simply on a treadmill indoors, came up with far more original ideas than those who stayed seated. The act of moving, not the scenery, appeared to make the difference.

None of this means we should abandon our desks completely. Careful, focused work still matters, and some tasks demand that we sit and concentrate. But when a problem feels stuck and the mind refuses to move, the oldest advice may also be the best: stand up, step outside, and let your feet do some of the thinking.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Walking can help the mind think more creatively and may help when a problem feels stuck.
Sitting at a desk is always better than walking for good ideas.
Only famous thinkers can benefit from going for a walk.
Beautiful scenery is the only reason walking helps our thinking.

questions.before: In the experiment, the people who walked came up with

questions.prompt: アイデアを生んだのは景色ではなく「動くこと」だと述べている語句はどれですか。

spans.text: more blood and oxygen reach the brain
The act of moving, not the scenery, appeared to make the difference
their best ideas came to them not at a desk but on a walk

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Walking is not a replacement for focused work, but it can free the mind when thinking feels stuck.
People should stop sitting at desks and only walk all day.
Creative ideas come only from beautiful outdoor scenery.
There is no real evidence that walking affects the mind.

listeningQuestions.transcript: Would you mind sending me the report before you leave the office today?
The train leaves from platform seven at a quarter to eleven every morning.
Had we known about the delay earlier, we would have taken a different route.
She has been studying abroad since the beginning of last September.
The big black bug bit the bare back of the brave boy by the barn.
Please remember to turn off the lights when you leave the meeting room.
